# Facebook Post — Over $160 Billion Raised by Three AI Companies in February 2026
# Week: Week of 2026-02-23
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation

---

Three AI companies just raised over $160 billion — in a single month.

February 2026 delivered the most concentrated wave of AI capital in history. OpenAI closed $110 billion from Amazon, SoftBank, and Nvidia — the largest private funding round ever recorded — at an $840 billion valuation. Anthropic raised $30 billion at a $380 billion valuation, with annual revenue running at $14 billion and growing roughly 10x year-over-year. xAI secured a $20 billion Series E backed by Nvidia, Cisco, and Saudi Arabia's Humain fund.

This is not a funding cycle. It's a capital reallocation event. Sovereign wealth funds, Big Tech treasuries, and semiconductor companies have collectively declared AI infrastructure the next dominant asset class.

What does this mean for executives? These war chests will fund aggressive enterprise pricing, rapid platform expansion, and infrastructure that compresses adoption timelines across every sector. Companies still treating AI as a pilot-stage initiative will find themselves competing against organizations backed by effectively unlimited capital.

The pace has changed. Strategic plans built on 12-month cycles are already outdated when the market moves in weeks.

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI raised $110B (from Amazon $50B, SoftBank $30B, Nvidia $30B) at $840B valuation — largest private funding round in history — MML Studio Feb 2026 recap
- Anthropic raised $30B (GIC, Coatue) at $380B valuation, with revenue at $14B/year representing ~10x year-over-year growth — MML Studio Feb 2026 recap
- xAI raised $20B Series E (Nvidia, Cisco, Saudi Humain contributing $3B) — MML Studio Feb 2026 recap
